intro: The deals actually worth your time from DansDeals and Doctor of Credit - sign-up bonuses you can hit with normal spend, easy bank bonuses, and whatever's hot right now. US and Australia. No fluff, no 'park $250k' nonsense.

items.name: Paze: up to $100 back + 10x points
items.detail: Up to $100 back per card + 10x points
items.meta: Barely any effort · going viral on DansDeals
items.why: Add your cards to Paze, pay with it where it's accepted, credits are already posting. Do it before it dies.

items.name: Chase Ink Business Cash
items.meta: $8k spend in 3 months · best offer ever
items.why: No annual fee, biggest bonus this card's ever had, and a business card doesn't touch your 5/24. Easiest grand going.

items.name: Chase Sapphire Preferred
items.why: The travel-points card I tell everyone to start with, and it's at the top of its offer range right now.

items.name: Chase IHG Rewards Premier
items.why: Elevated bonus plus a free anniversary night every year that pays the fee back on its own.

items.name: Qantas Amex Ultimate
items.meta: $5k spend in 3 months · apply by 28 July · $450 fee
items.why: First-timers (no Qantas card in 24 months) get another 10k on top. The $450 travel credit basically cancels the fee, so the points are close to free.

items.name: Chase Total Checking
items.meta: One direct deposit, no minimum · anyone online
items.why: About as easy as free money gets - open it, set one direct deposit, wait. No huge balance needed.

items.name: SoFi
items.why: Straightforward $300 for routing a direct deposit through it for a bit. Stack a shopping portal and it's even more.
